function.name: list_user_calendars
function.description: List all calendars associated with the authenticated Google account, along with their summaries and whether they are primary.

function.name: get_calendar_name
function.description: Retrieve the friendly display name (summary) of a specific calendar given its ID.

calendar_id.description: The unique ID of the target calendar (e.g., 'primary' or 'yourname@gmail.com').

function.name: get_calendar_timezone
function.description: Retrieve the default timezone (e.g., 'Asia/Shanghai', 'America/New_York') of the user's primary Google Calendar account to ensure correct local time formatting.

function.name: query_calendar
function.description: Query events across user calendars, optionally filtering by time range bounds and/or a free-text search term.

start_time.description: ISO 8601/RFC 3339 formatted lower bound (inclusive) for events to retrieve (e.g., '2026-05-23T00:00:00Z'). Optional.

end_time.description: ISO 8601/RFC 3339 formatted upper bound (exclusive) for events to retrieve (e.g., '2026-05-30T23:59:59Z'). Optional.

calendar_id.description: The ID of the calendar to query. Can be a single calendar ID, a comma-separated list of IDs, or 'all' to search across all of the user's calendars. Defaults to 'all'.

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 20.

text_query.description: Free-text search query to filter events by matching keywords in the title, description, or location. Optional.

function.name: get_calendar_event
function.description: Retrieve the full details of a specific single calendar event using its unique event ID.

calendar_id.description: The unique ID of the calendar containing the event.

event_id.description: The unique ID of the target event.

function.name: mutate_calendar_event
function.description: Perform write operations (insert, update, patch, delete) on a calendar event. Features an interactive terminal-based confirmation lock for security.

calendar_id.description: The unique ID of the target calendar. Defaults to 'primary'.

event_id.description: The unique ID of the event. Required for 'update', 'patch', and 'delete' actions.

event_body.description: The event details payload. Required for 'insert', 'update', and 'patch' actions. Must follow standard Google Calendar event resource format. For standard timed events, use 'dateTime' (e.g., {'dateTime': '2026-05-24T15:00:00+08:00'}). For all-day events, use 'date' (e.g., {'date': '2026-05-24'}) in YYYY-MM-DD format inside 'start' and 'end'. Note that for all-day events, the 'end' date is exclusive and represents the following day (e.g., for a 1-day event on June 1st, start is '2026-06-01' and end is '2026-06-02').

function.name: get_recurring_instances
function.description: Fetch all individual occurrences (instances) of a recurring event series.

master_event_id.description: The master recurring event series ID.

calendar_id.description: The unique ID of the target calendar. Defaults to 'primary'.

max_results.description: Maximum number of instances to return. Defaults to 20.

function.name: split_recurring_event
function.description: Split a recurring event series at a specific date. Truncates the old series and recreates a new series starting from the split date. Useful for 'update this and future occurrences'.

calendar_id.description: The unique ID of the target calendar.

master_id.description: The master recurring event ID to truncate.

split_date_str.description: The split effective date in YYYYMMDD format (e.g., '20260704').

new_event_body.description: The payload details of the new recurring series starting from the split date.

function.name: fetch_school_course_summary
function.description: Automatically execute the Blackboard scraper script ('cuhksz_bb_summary.py') in the sibling folder, retrieve the generated 'course_data_summary.txt' containing school course schedules, assignments, or deadlines, and return its textual content to the Agent.
